hacking.notes: Initial access via a leaked/abused GitHub access token (attacker-claimed by FulcrumSec; reported by multiple outlets). Token enabled access to additional credentials, then lateral movement across internal IT systems over 2+ months.

social.notes: Extortion: FulcrumSec demanded a reported $25M ransom; Novo Nordisk refused, after which the group said it was exploring private sales / leaking the data. The demand recipient and delivery channel were not specified, so target and vector are Unknown.

plus.analyst_notes:
Closes https://github.com/vz-risk/VCDB/issues/23372
Encoded by AI — encode-veris-incident skill version 20260618T195537Z

The PHIDBR2019 label was IGNORED per skill guidance (artifact/noise tag); the embedded '2019' is NOT the incident year — this breach is 2026.

ACTOR: FulcrumSec publicly claimed the attack. Coded actor.external.variety='Organized crime' and motive='Financial' from the ransom-and-sale extortion MO (a $25M demand followed by exploring private sales). actor.country=Unknown (not disclosed).

ACTION: action.hacking.variety='Use of stolen creds', vector='Web application' — entry was via a GitHub access token that yielded additional credentials (attacker-claimed by FulcrumSec, widely reported; not independently confirmed by Novo Nordisk in detail). result includes Infiltrate/Elevate/Lateral movement/Persist (2+ months dwell) and Exfiltrate (1.3TB claimed). action.social.variety='Extortion' for the $25M ransom demand that Novo refused; target and vector coded Unknown because the recipient/channel of the demand were not stated; attribute.integrity 'Alter behavior' and a 'P - Unknown' People asset accompany the social action per VERIS convention (the extortion attempt failed — Novo declined to pay).

ASSET: GitHub = S - Code repository (External Cloud); internal IT systems holding clinical-trial data = S - Database. cloud coded External Cloud + On-Premise (GitHub is cloud; internal IT systems' hosting not fully specified).

ATTRIBUTE: data_disclosure='Yes' — data confirmed copied/exfiltrated (Novo confirmed non-public data including personal data was copied; FulcrumSec claims 1.3TB). data varieties: Medical (biomarkers, immunogenicity/health data), Personal (pseudonymized patient IDs, sex, birth year, lifestyle factors; plus healthcare-provider names/registration/contact details), Secrets (drug R&D / internal proprietary data), Source code (internal AI model assets / GitHub contents). data is pseudonymized — not directly linked to patient names. state='Stored' (at rest; encryption status not stated). data_total OMITTED: only a claimed 1.3TB volume and 'a limited amount' of patient info are given — no defensible record/patient count across all victim groups. data_abuse='Yes - Data ransomed' (extortion + threatened sale/leak).

TIMELINE: incident begin = March 2026 (FulcrumSec says it accessed systems in March and spent 2+ months inside). Publicly disclosed by Novo on 2026-06-11 (issue opened 2026-06-15); discovery duration ~3 months. dbir_year=2026. discovery_method=external 'Actor disclosure' (surfaced via the extortion demand / leak threats).

VICTIM: Novo Nordisk, Danish pharmaceutical manufacturer (NAICS 31-33 label → 325412 Pharmaceutical Preparation Manufacturing). country=DK. ~77,000 employees → '50001 to 100000'.

SOURCES: securityweek.com, novonordisk.com (official incident update), bleepingcomputer.com, theregister.com, techrepublic.com, hipaajournal.com. Several specifics (1.3TB, $25M, GitHub-token entry, March access) are attacker-sourced (FulcrumSec) and not all victim-confirmed.

event_chain.summary: Initial access via a leaked/abused GitHub access token (Use of stolen creds)
Token used to obtain additional credentials; lateral movement and 2+ months of persistence
Clinical-trial data, AI model assets and internal data copied (claimed 1.3TB)
Extortion: $25M ransom demand (refused), then threatened private sale/leak

summary: Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk (maker of Ozempic and Wegovy) disclosed that attackers gained unauthorized access to internal IT systems and copied non-public data, including pseudonymized clinical-trial patient data (patient IDs, trial participation, sex, birth year, biomarkers, health/immunogenicity data, lifestyle factors), healthcare-provider contact details, internal AI model assets and other proprietary data. The hacking group FulcrumSec claimed responsibility, saying it entered via a GitHub access token in March 2026, dwelled for 2+ months, and exfiltrated a claimed 1.3TB. After Novo refused a reported $25M ransom, the group said it was exploring private sales of the data. Disclosed publicly on 2026-06-11.
